name: Organizations (Teams / IAM)
description: Teams / organizations (IAM) as a dregg-native cell. An Org is a factory-born cell owning a member→role roster; a role-cap is the org-owner's dregg-auth credential ATTENUATED to the role's permission set — so 'viewer is read-only' / 'admin cannot delete the org' is the SAME no-amplify lattice property dregg-auth already proves. Receipted membership turns: invite / accept (mints the member's attenuated role-cap) / remove / change-role / transfer-ownership. Supersedes a prior imperative org module.

heap_collections.MEMBER_COLL: 0x00000A61 — the witnessed member-subject roster (key i -> subject tag of member i, for i in 0..member_count)
heap_collections.ROLE_COLL: 0x00000A62 — the parallel member-role roster (key i -> role code of member i)

roles.owner: every permission (incl. org:delete, org:transfer, billing) — exactly one at a time
roles.admin: manage resources AND membership, see billing — NOT delete/transfer/pay

deos.mount: register_deos (src/lib.rs) — folds the composed DeosApp into the StarbridgeAppContext affordance registry

affordances.invite: cap-only, Signature (an admin offers a subject a role — pending)
affordances.accept: cap-only, Either (the invitee joins; the authority mints their attenuated role-cap)
affordances.remove: cap-only, Signature (an admin removes a member)
affordances.change_role: cap-only, Signature (an admin re-roles a member, re-issuing the cap)
affordances.transfer: GATED (cap∧state), root — precondition MEMBER_COUNT >= 2 (there must be a member to transfer TO); the fire submits the owner-moving turn (reading the LIVE seq + 1), the executor re-enforcing Monotonic(SEQ)

deos.seam: PARTIAL — the ROLE-CAP authorize (a viewer's members:manage attempt refused, unforgeably — the dregg-auth no-amplify lattice) and the cell invariants (WriteOnce(ROOT_PUBKEY/NAME) sealed identity/name + Monotonic(SEQ) append-only audit) are REAL. The roster's source of truth is the pure serializable Org record; the cell mirrors owner/member-count/seq into scalar slots and members into a committed heap image (a light client witnesses the roster). Threading each roster mutation through a per-member SetField allow-list program (so an off-roster write is itself an executor refusal) is the production lane this models.
